methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
Adarga is a Series B-stage Defense & Security company that has raised $26.2M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 firms across the sector—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private companies including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns, all at Series B stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression methodology uses total capital raised ($26.2M) as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $50M. Peer-median analysis examines the 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables, which trade at a median valuation of $1.2B with a range of $1.0B to $1.5B. This disparity reflects the wide distribution of performance and market positioning within the Defense & Security sector at Series B.

The triangulated analysis produces a range of $42M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $625M. The 33x spread between methods indicates material uncertainty. Stage-progression likely underweights Adarga's operational traction and market position relative to capital deployed. Peer-median methodology is more credible for this stage but carries significant model risk given the limited comparability of defense-tech businesses. Key uncertainties include revenue run rate, customer concentration, and defensibility of proprietary technology relative to the peer set.
